lineage: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
Concerning the fact that the divinity of the Word remained inseparable from the soul and the body, even at our Lord's death, and that His subsistence continued one.

metadata.author: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith

ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning the fact that the divinity of the Word remained inseparable from the soul and the body, even at our Lord's death, and that His subsistence continued one.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Since our Lord Jesus Christ was without sin (for He committed no sin, He Who took away the sin of the world, nor was there any deceit found in His mouth) He was not subject to death, since death came into the world through sin.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: He dies, therefore, because He took on Himself death on our behalf, and He makes Himself an offering to the Father for our sakes.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For we had sinned against Him, and it was meet that He should receive the ransom for us, and that we should thus be delivered from the condemnation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: God forbid that the blood of the Lord should have been offered to the tyrant.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Wherefore death approaches, and swallowing up the body as a bait is transfixed on the hook of divinity, and after tasting of a sinless and life-giving body, perishes, and brings up again all whom of old he swallowed up.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For just as darkness disappears on the introduction of light, so is death repulsed before the assault of life, and brings life to all, but death to the destroyer.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Wherefore, although He died as man and His Holy Spirit was severed from His immaculate body, yet His divinity remained inseparable from both, I mean, from His soul and His body, and so even thus His one hypostasis was not divided into two hypostases.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For body and soul received simultaneously in the beginning their being in the subsistence of the Word, and although they were severed from one another by death, yet they continued, each of them, having the one subsistence of the Word.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: So that the one subsistence of the Word is alike the subsistence of the Word, and of soul and body.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For at no time had either soul or body a separate subsistence of their own, different from that of the Word, and the subsistence of the Word is for ever one, and at no time two.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: So that the subsistence of Christ is always one.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For, although the soul was separated from the body topically, yet hypostatically they were united through the Word.
